title: SONIC: Supersizing Motion Tracking for Natural Humanoid Whole-Body Control
abstract: SONIC demonstrates that scaling motion tracking — using motion-capture data as direct policy supervision — produces a generalist humanoid controller with natural, dependable full-body movement. The approach scales along three axes: network parameters (1.2M to 42M), dataset scope (100M+ frames, 700 hours of human motion), and training compute (9k GPU hours). A real-time universal kinematic planner bridges motion tracking to downstream tasks, supporting VR teleoperation, video retargeting, and vision-language-action control within a single unified policy.
introduction_excerpt: We propose motion tracking as an efficient, scalable methodology for humanoid control, utilizing comprehensive supervision from motion-capture datasets to develop human motion understanding without requiring hand-crafted reward functions.
